sections.label: Canvas Session Lifecycle
decisions: Used flowchart to show the iterative session loop — the loop-back arrows from user choices to 'Announce' are the core insight

sections.label: Merge Architecture: How Sections Compose

annotations.text: Prefix isolates sections: auth_node1, dl_node1 — enables per-section tracking & deletion
Simple concat means parallel section-builders are merge-safe: no conflicts possible

decisions: Showed transform pipeline (prefix → position → merge) as sequential stages to make the order of operations clear
Used annotations to explain WHY concat is safe for parallel builders

sections.label: Session Resume: Reconciling State

annotations.text: If no sidecar exists: reconstruct from inspect output, ask user to confirm
Parallel reads: .excalidraw + sidecar at the same time

decisions: Used LR direction to contrast with the TB lifecycle above — emphasizes that resume is a linear pipeline, not a loop
Showed both discrepancy types (new unprefixed, missing prefixed) as separate branches to make the detection logic explicit

sections.label: 4-Mode Comparison: Explore vs Architect vs Storyboard vs Wireframe
decisions: Side-by-side columns make mode differences immediately scannable
Each column uses the same visual structure (steps → trait summary) so differences in content stand out
Color-coded per mode: green=explore, blue=architect, purple=storyboard, orange=wireframe

sections.label: Storyboard Mode — Deep Dive

annotations.text: Layout formula: frame_width = 500px, gap = 100px, x = i × 600
Prefix pattern: ph1_, ph2_, ph3_ — enables per-frame redo or deletion
Arrows carry the narrative forward: 'what happened' → 'what's next'

decisions: Zoomed into storyboard specifically because it has the most unique spatial layout strategy (LR frames with progression arrows)
Showed the frame iteration loop explicitly — the 'More frames?' diamond makes the sequential build pattern clear
Included the layout formula as an annotation so the positioning math is visible alongside the flow

findings: Canvas sessions use --merge/--position/--prefix flags to compose independent sections
canvas-inspect.js uses 200px grid scan to find free space
Sidecar .excalibrain.json tracks sections independently of .excalidraw format
Section-builder agents can run in parallel for architect mode
Resume reconciliation compares inspect output prefix groups against sidecar elementCount per section
No sidecar fallback: reconstruct from canvas-inspect and ask user to confirm
Explore = iterative/conversational, Architect = one-pass/parallel, Storyboard = sequential/narrative, Wireframe = screen-focused/UI
Storyboard layout: x = frame_index × (500 + 100), each frame gets ph<N>_ prefix, progression arrows at y-center between frames
